findings.title: Post-hydration proxy via sync-status-indicator is indirect — does not prove credential-banner evaluation completed in the same tick
findings.why: The replacement for waitForTimeout(500) waits for sync-status-indicator visibility as a hydration signal. This is an indirect proxy: the indicator rendering only proves the header shell mounted, not that useMissingCredentials has finished its async evaluation. Under a slow-hook scenario the banner could still materialize after the assertion window, producing a false-negative 'absent' assertion. The plan (R2) accepts this as 'deterministic or justified', and a justification comment is present, so this is not blocking.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider waiting directly on a banner-evaluation signal (e.g., a data-testid='credential-banner-evaluated' marker set after the hook settles) or increase to a scoped expect.poll against the banner locator with explicit timeout.

findings.title: Auto-close effect intentionally omits onClose from deps — eslint-react-hooks/exhaustive-deps may flag without a disable comment
findings.why: The effect body uses onCloseRef.current() while dependencies are [open, storeStatus, progress.done, progress.total, progress.processed]. There is a comment explaining why onClose is omitted, but no // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps directive. If that lint rule is enabled at error severity this will break CI. Confirmed the onCloseRef latch pattern is correct; this is purely a lint hygiene concern.
findings.suggestedFix: Add `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` immediately above the closing `}, [open, storeStatus, progress.done, progress.total, progress.processed])` line, or verify the project's eslint config does not enforce the rule.

findings.title: Wrapper remains focusable (tabIndex=0) after role removal — SR may announce as generic
findings.why: With showLabel=true the wrapper span drops role='img' but retains tabIndex={0}. Screen readers will announce the focusable span using the visible text label (correct), but some ATs may announce it as a generic focusable group rather than an image/status. aria-label is still present as fallback. This is consistent with R5's intent ('drops redundant role when labeled') but there's a minor semantic question about whether the tabIndex=0 makes sense on a status indicator at all.
findings.suggestedFix: Consider whether the badge should be focusable when it is purely decorative status. If keyboard users need to reach the Tooltip, keep tabIndex; otherwise drop it when showLabel=true.

findings.title: watchdogMs={50} real-timer test has mild flakiness risk under CI load
findings.why: The test uses real timers with watchdogMs=50 and waitFor timeout=500ms. Under heavy CI load or when the render path does any microtask work, the 10x margin may occasionally compress. Low probability but non-zero. The fake-timer tests (watchdogMs={0} and default) already cover the ?? vs || regression; the real-timer test is somewhat redundant.
findings.suggestedFix: Either (a) drop the real-timer 50ms test since fake-timer coverage suffices, or (b) bump waitFor timeout to 2000ms for CI headroom.

findings.title: Flow B popover test branches on banner presence — effectively a conditional no-op when banner is absent
findings.why: Flow B's assertion body is inside `if (bannerCount > 0)`. In practice the test environment's seeded missing-credentials state determines which branch runs; if the test always hits the else-branch it exercises only the banner-absence assertion, never the popover content itself. The plan's R3-(b) intent ('Why? popover content rendered') is only validated in the if-branch.
findings.suggestedFix: Either seed a missing-credential entry explicitly in beforeEach (so the if-branch always runs), or split into two tests gated on explicit state.

findings.title: Adding user?.id to deps churns handleSyncNow on any user-object re-reference if selector isn't shallow-stable
findings.why: The comment claims `user?.id` is added because 'user' itself would churn on every render due to the selector returning a new reference. However, Zustand's default selector behavior returns the same reference for primitive-valued selectors, and for object selectors uses Object.is. If the store actually returns a new user object each render (e.g., via `s => ({ ...s.user })`), then `user?.id` stays stable because id is a primitive. This is fine. The concern would only arise if some mutation path replaces the user object with a same-id-but-new-reference, which would NOT trigger the dep change for `user?.id`. So the optimization is correct, but the comment's reasoning could mislead future readers.
findings.suggestedFix: No code change required. Optionally refine the comment to say 'user?.id is a primitive and stable across selector re-references' rather than implying 'user' is inherently unstable.
